channel.title: Simple IT SRQ Blog
channel.description: Insights for Sarasota and Bradenton businesses on cybersecurity, AI, cloud, and compliance.

item.title: AI Comprehension Debt: A Hidden Risk for SRQ IT Teams
item.description: AI coding tools speed teams up, but quietly erode understanding. Heres how Sarasota and Bradenton businesses can avoid comprehension debt.

item.title: Gamified Tech Training: A Smarter Way to Onboard SRQ Staff
item.description: An interactive browser game teaches users to build a GPU from logic gates up. Heres how Sarasota businesses can borrow the format for IT onboarding.

item.title: AI Vendor Lock-In: Update Your Bradenton Procurement Playbook
item.description: Anthropic blocked third-party tools from paid Claude Code accounts. Heres what that means for AI vendor risk in your Bradenton business.

item.title: LinkedIn Is Fingerprinting Your Browser. Heres What to Do.
item.description: Researchers caught LinkedIn probing browser extensions for fingerprinting. What it means for SRQ businesses and how to harden client browsers.

item.title: Evaluating Agentic Coding Tools: A Visual Primer for IT
item.description: A new visual guide unpacks how Claude Codes agent loop, tool use, and context window actually work. Heres how SRQ IT teams should use it for evaluation.

item.title: The Claude Code Source Map Leak: Clean Up Your Build Pipeline
item.description: A source map shipped with an NPM package exposed Anthropics Claude Code internals. Heres what every Sarasota dev shop should add to their CI pipeline.

item.title: AI Assistants Now Inject Ads. Audit Your Supply Chain.
item.description: A developer caught GitHub Copilot inserting promotional language into a PR comment. Heres how Sarasota businesses should treat AI output in code review.

item.title: Bot Detections Hidden Privacy Cost for SRQ SaaS Users
item.description: ChatGPTs Cloudflare Turnstile inspects React state before letting you type. Heres what bot detection is costing your Sarasota SaaS privacy.

item.title: Founder Succession Planning for Bradenton Small Businesses
item.description: GitLabs founder went public about his cancer diagnosis. Heres what owner-dependency risk looks like for Bradenton SMBs and how IT plays a role.

item.title: Opt Out by April 24: GitHub Copilot Training on Private Repos
item.description: GitHub will train Copilot on private repos by default unless you opt out by April 24, 2026. Heres what Sarasota businesses need to do this week.

item.title: EU Rejects Chat Control: A Big Privacy Win, Briefly
item.description: The EU Parliament rejected mandatory client-side scanning. Heres what that means for end-to-end encryption and Sarasota businesses with EU customers.

item.title: Why Encryption Backdoors Break Security for SRQ Businesses
item.description: The Fight Chat Control campaign explains why mandated scanning breaks security for everyone. Heres the SRQ-business version of that argument.

item.title: Wine 11 Closes the Gap: Cheaper Workstation Refreshes for SMBs
item.description: Wine 11 brings native-class performance to Linux workstations running Windows software. Heres what that means for SRQ businesses planning a refresh.

item.title: Data Sovereignty: When Florida Businesses Look to EU Hosting
item.description: A founder documented moving infrastructure to EU providers. Heres how Sarasota businesses should think about data sovereignty and US vendor risk.

item.title: Post-Git Version Control: Should SRQ Engineering Teams Care?
item.description: Bram Cohen lays out what should come after Git: better merges, content addressing, large files. Heres what it means for SRQ engineering leaders.

item.title: Ubuntu 26.04 Breaks 46 Years of Sudo Habit. Test Before Upgrade.
item.description: Ubuntu 26.04 changes sudo to provide audible password feedback. A small UX change that breaks ancient automation. Test your provisioning scripts.

item.title: OpenCode and the Case for Open-Source AI in SRQ Workshops
item.description: OpenCode is a fully open-source alternative to Claude Code and Cursor. Heres why SRQ businesses should consider it when data cant leave the building.

item.title: When Beloved OSS Gets Acquired: Vendor Risk for Sarasota IT
item.description: Astral, the team behind uv and ruff, was acquired by OpenAI. Heres how SRQ businesses should think about OSS acquisitions and pinning tools.

item.title: Rob Pikes 1989 Rules That Still Drive Smart IT Decisions
item.description: Rob Pikes five rules of programming resurfaced this week. Heres how they apply to modern IT decisions for Sarasota and Bradenton businesses.

item.title: Style-Transfer LLMs Have Arrived. Now What for SRQ Marketing?
item.description: Kagis joke translator that converts plain English to LinkedIn Speak is also a real demo of style transfer. Heres what it means for Sarasota marketing teams.

item.title: Prediction Markets, Information Warfare, and Your Brand Risk
item.description: A reporter received death threats from prediction-market traders trying to sway his coverage. Heres what that means for SRQ businesses doing public research.

item.title: Cross-Border Data Rules: Why SRQ Companies Should Care
item.description: Canadas Bill C-22 returns with surveillance backdoor risks. Heres what cross-border data rules mean for Sarasota and Bradenton businesses.

item.title: Locked-Down Linux for Front-Desk and Kiosk Deployments
item.description: Ageless Linux is designed for non-technical users with large fonts and tamper resistance. Heres how SRQ businesses can use it for kiosk deployments.

item.title: Can You Run AI On-Prem? A Practical Guide for Bradenton SMBs
item.description: A new tool detects your hardware and tells you which open-weight models you can run locally. Heres a practical guide for Bradenton SMBs.

item.title: Breaking Free from Legacy Vendor Lock-In: Clean-Room Migrations
item.description: A new service productizes clean-room reverse engineering for OSS interop. Heres what that means for SRQ businesses migrating off legacy vendors.

item.title: Drawing the Line on AI-Generated Content at Work
item.description: Hacker News updated its guidelines to ban AI-generated comments. Heres what an AI content policy looks like for a Sarasota or Bradenton business.

item.title: Tony Hoares Lessons That Still Matter for SRQ IT Leaders
item.description: Tony Hoare passed away at 91. Heres what his work on Quicksort, null references, and CSP still teaches Sarasota and Bradenton IT leaders.

item.title: Are AI Coding Tools Really a Money Pit? A Sarasota Reality Check
item.description: A detailed cost analysis pushed back on viral claims that Anthropic loses thousands per Claude Code user. Heres what it means for SMB AI budgeting.

item.title: Sandboxing AI Coding Agents: A Safer Default for SRQ Teams
item.description: Agent Safehouse wraps AI coding agents in macOS sandboxing. Heres why SRQ businesses should sandbox every agent that touches files or shells.

item.title: A Decade of Docker: What Containers Mean for Bradenton SMBs
item.description: A CACM retrospective on how Docker containers reshaped software delivery. Heres what containerization means for Sarasota and Bradenton SMBs in 2026.

item.title: Tech Layoffs Mean Better IT Hiring for Sarasota Businesses
item.description: Tech sector job losses now exceed the 2008 and 2020 recessions. Heres what that means for Sarasota and Bradenton businesses hiring IT talent in 2026.
